level1_segments.summary: The circulator nurse assists the scrub nurse and holds surgical instruments during the initial phase of the procedure.
The circulator nurse intermittently holds surgical instruments with brief pauses in between.
The circulator nurse engages in a long period of holding surgical instruments interspersed with short idle gaps.
The circulator nurse manipulates the operating table, prepares the patient, and assists the assistant surgeon.

The circulator nurse performs final technical tasks including C-arm manipulation, table adjustment, and instrument handoffs to conclude the procedure.

level1_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient site with brief pauses and touches.
The head surgeon performs drilling on the patient while alternating with general instrument handling and touching.
The head surgeon calibrates the Mako robotic arm with intermittent pauses and patient contact.
The head surgeon cuts the patient tissue and then engages in extended touching of the patient site.
The head surgeon calibrates and manipulates the Mako robotic arm while holding a bone saw.
The head surgeon uses the bone saw to cut the patient while manipulating the robotic arm, with brief pauses for instrument adjustment.
The head surgeon uses a bone saw and the Mako robotic arm to perform a sawing procedure on the patient.
The head surgeon drills into the patient's bone and then briefly touches the surgical site.
After preparing the instrument table, the surgeon repeatedly hammers the patient and manipulates surgical instruments at the site.
The head surgeon resumes activity by touching the patient, hammering, and continuing to touch the surgical site.

level2_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient site and performs initial drilling to ready the bone for further intervention.
The surgeon calibrates the Mako robotic arm and utilizes it alongside a bone saw to perform precise tissue and bone resection.
The head surgeon completes bone preparation with drilling and proceeds to hammer instruments and manipulate the site for implant placement.

level1_segments.summary: The surgical instrument is held by the robot technician during the initial phase of the procedure.
The instrument undergoes a prolonged period of handling and transfer between the circulator nurse, scrub nurse, and robot technician as it is prepared and staged for use.
The instrument is transferred to the head surgeon and actively used during the surgical intervention.

The instrument undergoes a series of rapid exchanges between the scrub nurse and the head surgeon.

The instrument is held by the scrub nurse, then transferred to the circulator nurse, the robot technician, and back to the scrub nurse for final handling.

The instrument is actively utilized by the head surgeon for the main surgical intervention, involving multiple exchanges with the scrub nurse.

level1_segments.summary: The robot technician performs a series of alternating manipulations of the Mako robotic arm and the MPS workstation to calibrate and prepare the system.
The technician resumes work by manipulating the robotic arm and workstation while briefly holding a surgical instrument.
The technician performs final adjustments by alternating between the MPS workstation and the Mako robotic arm with brief idle gaps.
The robot technician manipulates the robotic arm and briefly checks the workstation before a short pause.
After a long idle period, the technician holds a surgical instrument and then pauses again.
The technician interacts with the MPS workstation, takes a break, and then resumes workstation manipulation.
The technician touches and prepares the patient, followed by a long period of inactivity.
Following a pause, the technician briefly uses the workstation and then prepares the patient.
The technician pauses, briefly manipulates the workstation, and then enters a long idle period.
The technician handles a surgical drape and prepares the patient, pauses, and then uses the workstation.
After a pause, the technician manipulates the workstation and then experiences a long idle period.
The technician prepares the instrument table, pauses, and then manipulates the infrared tracker.
The robot technician performs an extended session of manipulating the MPS workstation and robotic arm with brief pauses.
The technician engages in intermittent manipulation of the MPS workstation separated by short idle periods.
The robot technician alternates between manipulating the Mako robotic arm and the MPS workstation with brief pauses.
The technician holds a surgical instrument and briefly manipulates the MPS workstation after a long idle period.
The robot technician held a surgical instrument, briefly paused, and then manipulated the MPS workstation.
After a brief pause, the robot technician prepared the instrument table.

level2_segments.summary: The robot technician performs initial system calibration and preparation by alternating between the Mako robotic arm and the MPS workstation.
The technician engages in intermittent patient preparation, instrument handling, and workstation checks separated by periods of inactivity.
An extended phase of active robotic surgery occurs where the technician continuously manipulates the MPS workstation and robotic arm.
The technician concludes the session by handling surgical instruments, performing final workstation manipulations, and preparing the instrument table.

The scrub nurse continuously holds surgical instruments and prepares the instrument table with short idle gaps.

level1_segments.summary: The robot monitor is started and then finished, indicating an initial system check or brief interaction.

A comprehensive pre-surgery calibration sequence is performed, including arm status checks, homing, accuracy verification, brake and angle discrepancy checks, culminating in a successful completion status.
Initial bone registration and surgery planning steps are initiated on the robot monitor.
An extended bone registration process occurs, characterized by multiple interruptions due to patient timeouts.
Detailed bone registration is completed for specific anatomical regions including distal, posterior, anterior, and tibial surfaces.
The registration phase is finalized and the system is restarted for the next stage of the procedure.

level2_segments.summary: The robot system undergoes initial startup and a comprehensive pre-surgery calibration sequence to verify arm status and accuracy.
The surgical team performs an extended bone registration process covering multiple anatomical surfaces and finalizes the planning phase before restarting the system.

level1_segments.summary: The robot is powered on and undergoes initial calibration by the MPS.
The robot is dressed to ensure it is sterile for the procedure.
The nurse installs the base and calibration arrays, performs robot calibration, and then removes the calibration array.
The actual saw tip is installed onto the robot.

level2_segments.summary: The robot is powered on, calibrated, and dressed to achieve a sterile state for the procedure.
The nurse installs and calibrates the base arrays, followed by the installation of the surgical saw tip.
